title: centaurea phrygia subsp. rarauensis
intro: Centaurea phrygia subsp. rarauensis, commonly known as the Phrygian centaury, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Greece and Turkey. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea phrygia subsp. rarauensis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress. Centaurea phrygia subsp. rarauensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in the form of dried flowers and leaves.
